# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-05-21T11:47:03Z

## Testing Standards

- pytest is the test framework. Tests live in tests/ and currently number 25 passing tests, including a real-export regression for Health Connect, fixture-based parser unit tests, an age round-trip suite, and a launchd plist plutil-lint test. No formal coverage floor is enforced; new parsers and any code that touches health data should ship with at least one fixture-based test, and pytest must be green before any change is handed off for review. TDD is not mandated. The @pytest.mark.regression suite is allowed to be skipped when real export files are absent.


## Quality Gates

- Before review handoff: ruff (lint + format) clean, mypy clean for the changed scope, pytest -q green. Pre-existing failures unrelated to the change must be called out explicitly, not hidden. No pre-commit hook is required, but the same gates run locally are the de-facto merge gate. Static type hints are expected on public functions; runtime input validation lives at the parser/CLI boundary (pydantic, defensive PRAGMA table_info checks for the HC parser).


## Performance Benchmarks

- Soft targets from SPEC §4 NFR-Performance, not pass/fail: full HC ingest (~200 MB export) under 60 s on M-series Mac; monthly end-to-end run (Garmin GDPR included) under 10 minutes for a ~500 MB zip; resulting DuckDB warehouse under 1 GB before encryption after five years of history. CLI commands should respond in under 2 s for the non-ingest verbs (status, doctor, gc).


## Branch Strategy

- Single-maintainer project: the operator is the only reviewer today and self-reviews via the spec-kitty implement-review loop (kitty-specs/* work packages). External contributions arrive as GitHub PRs and require at least one maintainer approval plus all quality gates green before merge. CI is not currently configured; review correctness is the merge gate. Conventional commits are NOT required; commit messages should be descriptive and reference the spec-kitty work package or doctrine action when applicable.

- Deployment constraints: Primary platform: macOS (launchd-scheduled monthly run). Linux/Windows are out of v1 scope but the warehouse file and encrypted artifacts MUST remain platform-portable (stock age + DuckDB ≥1.1). master is the single long-lived branch; feature work happens in spec-kitty worktrees under .worktrees/ and is merged back to master via the spec-kitty merge workflow. Release artifacts are git tags (v1.0.0 is the current release); semver applies once external users exist. No CI/CD, no container, no server-side deploy — this is a CLI tool installed via uv.


## Governance Activation

```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: []
selected_directives: [DIRECTIVE_010, DIRECTIVE_024, DIRECTIVE_028, DIRECTIVE_030, DIRECTIVE_033]
available_tools: [spec-kitty, git]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

## Policy Summary

- Intent: Premura is a personal-health-data warehouse and pipeline (Python 3.11, uv, DuckDB, age, rclone) that ingests monthly GDPR-style dumps from Garmin Connect, Android Health Connect, Sleep as Android, and Body Measurement Tracker into a single locally-owned encrypted DuckDB. The project shipped v1 on 2026-05-21 and is now being hardened for open-source release while remaining a single-operator system. The full functional and non-functional spec (including the GDPR Article 9 security posture and the encrypt-before- upload guarantee) lives in docs/SPEC.md and is the authoritative source of system requirements; this charter governs how we work, not what the system does.

- Languages/Frameworks: Python 3.11 (single language, no frontend). Core libraries: typer + rich (CLI), pydantic + pydantic-settings (config), DuckDB 1.1, polars (parse + transform), structlog (logging), jinja2 (launchd plist), pyarrow, python-ulid. Dev tooling: uv (package + virtualenv manager), pytest + pytest-cov, ruff (lint + format), mypy (types). No other languages or frameworks are in scope.

- Testing: pytest is the test framework. Tests live in tests/ and currently number 25 passing tests, including a real-export regression for Health Connect, fixture-based parser unit tests, an age round-trip suite, and a launchd plist plutil-lint test. No formal coverage floor is enforced; new parsers and any code that touches health data should ship with at least one fixture-based test, and pytest must be green before any change is handed off for review. TDD is not mandated. The @pytest.mark.regression suite is allowed to be skipped when real export files are absent.

- Quality Gates: Before review handoff: ruff (lint + format) clean, mypy clean for the changed scope, pytest -q green. Pre-existing failures unrelated to the change must be called out explicitly, not hidden. No pre-commit hook is required, but the same gates run locally are the de-facto merge gate. Static type hints are expected on public functions; runtime input validation lives at the parser/CLI boundary (pydantic, defensive PRAGMA table_info checks for the HC parser).

- Review Policy: Single-maintainer project: the operator is the only reviewer today and self-reviews via the spec-kitty implement-review loop (kitty-specs/* work packages). External contributions arrive as GitHub PRs and require at least one maintainer approval plus all quality gates green before merge. CI is not currently configured; review correctness is the merge gate. Conventional commits are NOT required; commit messages should be descriptive and reference the spec-kitty work package or doctrine action when applicable.

- Performance Targets: Soft targets from SPEC §4 NFR-Performance, not pass/fail: full HC ingest (~200 MB export) under 60 s on M-series Mac; monthly end-to-end run (Garmin GDPR included) under 10 minutes for a ~500 MB zip; resulting DuckDB warehouse under 1 GB before encryption after five years of history. CLI commands should respond in under 2 s for the non-ingest verbs (status, doctor, gc).

- Deployment Constraints: Primary platform: macOS (launchd-scheduled monthly run). Linux/Windows are out of v1 scope but the warehouse file and encrypted artifacts MUST remain platform-portable (stock age + DuckDB ≥1.1). master is the single long-lived branch; feature work happens in spec-kitty worktrees under .worktrees/ and is merged back to master via the spec-kitty merge workflow. Release artifacts are git tags (v1.0.0 is the current release); semver applies once external users exist. No CI/CD, no container, no server-side deploy — this is a CLI tool installed via uv.


## Project Directives

1. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_010` to planning and implementation decisions.
2. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_024` to planning and implementation decisions.
3. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_028` to planning and implementation decisions.
4. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_030` to planning and implementation decisions.
5. Apply doctrine directive `DIRECTIVE_033` to planning and implementation decisions.
6. Respect risk boundaries: Three non-negotiable governance boundaries for this project: (1) No live API scraping — ingestion uses official user-initiated exports only (GDPR dumps, HC auto-export files, app-export CSVs). python-garminconnect-style logins, Google Fit REST, and Apple HealthKit are out of scope. (2) Local-first / offline by default — the pipeline MUST run end-to-end with no network. Drive upload is opt-in (hpipe upload), never part of the automated monthly run. hpipe doctor and all read paths MUST NOT phone home. (3) Single-operator assumption — the system is built for one operator who is also the data subject. Multi-user, shared-account, and multi-tenant features are explicitly out of scope; PRs that introduce them are rejected. These complement (and do not replace) the security NFRs in SPEC §4 (age-encryption mandatory before any cloud upload, age.key chmod 0600, no PHI in logs/tests/commits).

7. Keep documentation synchronized with workflow and behavior changes.

Reduces brittle tests while ensuring meaningful validation. | `_LIBRARY/tactic-test-boundaries-by-responsibility.md` |

 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-test-pyramid-progression.md` |

Avoids both over-testing low-risk changes and under-testing high-risk ones. Grounded in the test pyramid model where ROI decreases as test scope widens.
 | `_LIBRARY/tactic-testing-select-appropriate-level.md` |

## Amendment Process

The charter is amended by editing .kittify/charter/charter.md directly and running `spec-kitty charter sync --json`. Material amendments (changing a risk boundary, lowering a quality gate, adding/removing a directive) MUST be committed in a dedicated commit whose message starts with "charter:" and links to the spec-kitty mission or issue that motivated the change. Editorial fixes (typos, clarifications) may ride in any commit. The interview answers in this file are the editable source for regeneration; if the charter diverges from these answers, regenerate rather than hand-edit downstream YAML.


## Exception Policy

Quality-gate exceptions (e.g., a known-failing mypy line that is pre-existing scope) MUST be called out explicitly in the work package or PR description with a one-line rationale; silent skips are not permitted. Risk-boundary exceptions (live API access, multi-user surface, online-only behavior) are not allowed under this charter — a feature that requires breaching one of the three risk boundaries requires a charter amendment first. SPEC-level security requirements (encryption, key permissions) admit no exceptions.
